content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 24, 2009

Chris Hansen
Director of Planning and Outreach
Councilmember Carl DeMaio, 5th District
202 C Street
San Diego, CA 92101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-078

Dear Mr. Hansen:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Carl DeMaio, Councilmember for the City of San Diego ("City"), for advice regarding the mass mailing at public expense provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

1. Does the enclosed proposed mass mailing permit the one mention of Councilmember Carl DeMaio's office and name?

2. Does the enclosed proposed mass mailing permit inclusion of the word "our" in the phrase: "Come join a discussion of the ways to improve our neighborhood?"

3. Does the mass mailing prohibition in the Act prohibit the inclusion of references to the councilmember's district in a publically funded mailings?

CONCLUSIONS

1. and 2. Generally, Regulation 18901 prohibits the use of an agency's elected official's name, or any other reference to the elected official or his or her office, in a mass mailing by the agency, unless, as discussed below, an exception applies.

_______________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

Our File No. A-09-078
Page 2

3. Yes. Absent a specific exception, as discussed below, a reference to the
councilmember's district is considered a reference to his or her office.

FACTS

San Diego City Councilmember DeMaio wishes to send a mailing inviting constituents to
a neighborhood meeting. It also asks recipients to complete a survey regarding city issues. The
mailing includes the following references to the councilmember and/or his office:

(1) "City Councilmember Carl DeMaio invites you" on the front of the mailer.

(2) The website address "www.sandiego.gov/citycouuncil/cd5" is included in the contact
information on the back page and in a footer on the back side next to the description of the
location of the event.

(3) Use of the word "our" in the following phrase at the bottom of the front page in the
mailing: "Come join a discussion of ways to improve our neighborhood."

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 of the Act provides that "no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent
at public expense." Regulation 18901(a) states in pertinent part:

"(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is prohibited by section
89001 if all of the following criteria are met:

"(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her
residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. For purposes of
this subdivision (a)(1), the item delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item,
such as a videotape, record, or button, or a written document.

"(2) The item sent either:

"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, or

"(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is
prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer.

"(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public moneys; or

Our File No. A-09-078
Page 3

"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $ 50.00 are paid with
public moneys, and the design, production, or printing is done with the intent of
sending the item other than as permitted by this regulation.

"(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in a single
calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request and
any item described in subdivision (b)."

Your question presupposes that more than 200 copies of the item will be mailed or
delivered, by any means, to homes, offices, and/or post office boxes in a calendar month, and
will be created and sent with public funds. Your question concerns subdivision (a)(2) which
provides that for an item to be prohibited, the item must either:

"Feature an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends
the mailing, or
"Include the name, office, photograph, or other reference to an elected officer
affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected
officer."

Clearly, the mailing in question "features" the councilmember. Regulation 18901(c)
defines "features an elected officer" to mean that the item mailed includes the elected officer's
photograph or signature, or singles out the elected officer by the manner of display of his or her
name or office in the layout of the document, such as by headlines, captions, type size, typeface,
or type color. In addition, the proposed mailing includes a personal pronoun ("our") which is a
reference to the councilmember, and refers to the councilmember's district ("cd5") in the website
address listed with other contact information on the first page of the proposed mailing and in a
footer on the back side of the mailer.

Exceptions

Under Section 89001 and Regulation 18901 any item mailed that meets the definition of
mass mailing discussed above may not include the elected officer's name or photograph or any
reference to his or her office unless a specific exception applies. The sample item you sent
contains the following reference to Councilmember DeMaio or his office:

(1) "City Councilmember Carl DeMaio invites you" on the front of the mailer.

(2) Use of the word "our" in the following phrase at the bottom of the first page of the
mailer: "Come join a discussion of ways to improve our neighborhood."

One of the exceptions included in Regulation 18901 pertains to the sending of constituent
meeting notices. (Regulation 18901(b)(9)(A)(1).) Under this exception, mass mailing of

Our File No. A-09-078
Page 4

the following is not prohibited by Section 89001:

"(A) An announcement of any meeting or event of the type listed in paragraphs 1
or 2.

"1. An announcement sent to an elected officer's constituents concerning a public
meeting which is directly related to the elected officer's incumbent governmental
duties, which is to be held by the elected officer, and which the elected officer
intends to attend.

"2. An announcement of any official agency event or events for which the agency
is providing the use of its facilities or staff or other financial support.

"(B) Any announcement provided for in this subdivision (b)(9) shall not include
the elected officer's photograph or signature and may include only a single
mention of the elected officer's name except as permitted elsewhere in this
regulation." (Regulation 18901(b)(9)(A) and (B).)

Reviewing the item, the use of the councilmember's name (including his official title
used in conjunction with his name) in this instance fits this exception.

However, we have previously advised that this exception does not permit any other
reference to the elected officer unless another exception applies. (Regulation 18901(b)(9)(B).)
In this regard, we have previously advised that use of a personal pronoun in a mailer that refers
to the elected officer, either individually, or as part of a group (e.g., "I," "me," "we," "us," "our")
constitutes an impermissible second reference to the elected official. (de Mayo Advice Letter,
No. I-08-125.) Here the mailer includes the word "our" and this use can be construed as
referring to the councilmember. Therefore, since no other exception applies, the mailer cannot
contain the word "our."

(2) The website address "www.sandiego.gov/citycoun cil/cd5" is included in the contact
information on the back page and in a footer on the back side next to the description of the
location of the event.

You asked whether reference to a website address, which contains a reference to the
councilmember's district, would be considered a reference to the councilmember. In the past, we
have advised that a reference to the "city council" or other body would not be a reference to the
elected officers that serve on that body. (Lipton Advice Letter, No. A-92-476.) However, a
reference to city council district 5 (or cd5) is a reference to a specific office and is prohibited by
Section 89001 unless an exception applies. Generally, the fact that the reference is part of a web
site address does not affect this conclusion. (Houston Advice Letter, No. I-05-183.)

However, subdivision (b)(1) of Regulation 18901 permits an elected officer's name to
appear in the letterhead or logotype of his or her agency in a mass mailing. We have included
within this exception other references to the elected officer that appear in the agency letterhead

Our File No. A-09-078
Page 5

such as his or her office or district, or inclusion of the officer's name in his or her electronic mail
address. (White-Brown Advice Letter, No. A-96-097) The bottom left on the first page of the
mailer includes a listing of the councilmember's address, telephone number, electronic mail
address and his website address. Inclusion of an official's website address as part of an
organization's letterhead or logotype is a common and expected feature of modern society.
Accordingly, we think its inclusion in the letterhead or logotype of a government agency is
permissible under Section 89001 and Regulation 18901, even if the website address makes
reference to the district an elected officer of an agency represents.

However, reference to this website address in the footer on the back side of the mailer, or
in any other part of the mailer, other than the letterhead or logotype of the government agency
cannot be included in the mailer.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

[illegible signature]

By:   John W. Wallace
      Assistant General Counsel
      Legal Division

sections.conclusion:
1. and 2. Generally, Regulation 18901 prohibits the use of an agency's elected official's
name, or any other reference to the elected official or his or her office, in a mass mailing by the
agency, unless, as discussed below, an exception applies.
Our Page2
3. Yes. Absent a specific exception, as discussed below, a reference to the
councilmember's district is considered a reference to his or her office.

89001 if all of the following criteria are met:

residence, place of employment or business, or post office box. For purposes of

such as a videotape, record, or button, or a written document.

"(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, or

"(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $ 50.00 are paid with

the mailing, or

affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared

(2) Use of the word "our" in the following phrase at the bottom of the first page of the
mailer: "Come join a discussion of ways to improve our neighborhood."
One of the exceptions included in Regulation 18901 pertains to the sending of constituent

Our Page 4
the following is not prohibited by Section 89001:
"(A) An announcement of any meeting or event of the type listed in paragraphs I
or 2.
"1. An announcement sent to an elected officer's constituents concerning a public
meeting which is directly related to the elected officer's incumbent governmental
duties, which is to be held by the elected officer, and which the elected officer
intends to attend.
*2. An announcement of any official agency event or events for which the agency
is providing the use of its facilities or staff or other financial support.
"(B) Any announcement provided for in this subdivision (bX9) shall not include
the elected officer's photograph or signature and may include only a single
mention of the elected officer's name except as permitted elsewhere in this
regulation." (Regulation 18901(bXg)(A) and (B).)
Reviewing the item, the use of the councilmember's name (including his official title
used in conjunction with his name) in this instance fits this exception.
However, we have previously advised that this exception does not permit any other
reference to the elected officer unless another exception applies. (Regulation lSg0libXgXB).)
In this regard, we have previously advised that use of a personal pronoun in a mailer that refers

constitutes an impermissible second reference to the elected official. (de MayoAdvice Letter,
No. I-08-125.) Here the mailer includes the word "our" and this use can be construed as

such as his or her office or district, or inclusion of the officer's name in his or her electronic mail

However, reference to this website address in the footer on the back side of the mailer, or
